name: Johnny Pacheco
abstract: Juan Pablo Knipping Pacheco (25 March 1935 – 15 February 2021), known as Johnny Pacheco, was a Dominican musician, arranger, composer, bandleader, and record producer. Born in the Dominican Republic, Pacheco became a leading figure in the New York salsa scene in the 1960s and 1970s as the founder and musical director of Fania Records.

concerts.label: JVC Jazz Festival: Latin Jazz Jam '89
JVC Jazz Festival: Latin Jazz Jam '89

about_quotes.speaker: Onaje Allan Gumbs
about_quotes.evidence: my sister also had an impact on me because she had a very eclectic taste as a teenager. ... she had Barbra Streisand, Johnny Mathis, Igor Stravinsky, Sonny Rollins, Loretta Alexandria, Johnny Pacheco, you know it was just endless
about_quotes.doc_title: Onaje Allan Gumbs interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Malone
about_quotes.evidence: I played on all the salsa recordings in New York too. Fania Records, 1974, ’75, ’76. Willie Colon, Hector Lavoe, Ruben Blades, Johnny Pacheco, Mongo Santamaria...
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom Malone interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 3, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: George Benson
about_quotes.evidence: So he got Ray Barretto. Couldn’t beat it. No, it wasn’t Ray. It was Johnny Pacheco. Johnny just made things even out so beautifully for me, made it easy.
about_quotes.doc_title: George Benson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Palmieri
about_quotes.evidence: Pacheco plays flute. The album comes out... Johnny Pacheco sells 100,000 45s... Manny Oquendo had been playing with the charanga of Pacheco... David Perez is coming out of the service... who played with Pacheco on bass
about_quotes.doc_title: Eddie Palmieri — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
